titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 47

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=146 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:03.719339+00 | translation moa=147 status=live modified=2016-06-07 14:47:47.651369+00

div.p:
Vniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis Walterus iuuenis de Rokesburg’ . salutem in domino . Nouerit uniueritas uestra me concessisse ⁊ quietas clamasse Rannulfo archidiacono de sancto andrea ⁊ successoribus
eius inperpetuum . sconin . ⁊ balekathelin . ⁊ terram quam iacet ex australi parte urbis sancti andree . inter torrentes quas terras pie memorie Walterus archidiaconus eiusdem loci auunculus meus tenuit ⁊ possedit in uita sua .
quas etiam post eius tempora ego ipse habui ⁊ possedi . Resignaui etiam predicto . R . archidiacono totum ius quod in predictis terris habui . siue ex dono . siue ex concessione . siue ex confirmatione pie recordationis episcopi
hugonis . siue auunculi mei Walteri archidiaconi . ⁊ cartas quas de terris illis habui  eidem prenominato . R . reddidi cum scirem illas ad archidiaconatum sancti andree pertinere . ⁊ ut hec concessio ⁊ resignatio
rata sit ⁊ inconcussa infuturum  illas corroboraui appositione sigillorum domini . R . Brethinensis episcopi . ⁊ H . abbatis de aberbruthot . ⁊ mei . dependentium a presenti pagina . volens tenori eius ⁊ continentie
fidem haberi in posterum . his testibus . R . episcopo brethinensi . H . abbati de aberbruthot . ⁊ conuentu eiusdem loci . ... archidiacono brethinensi . Willelmo ⁊ petro capellanis domini brethinensi . Henrico ca
pellano de munros . Hervico persona de insula brioci . Alano clerico de munros . Rogero de Inuerkethin . Herberto de munros . Willelmo filio Ede . Sthephano le mare. Roberto de
Hales . ⁊ multis aliis .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Walter Young of Roxburgh sends greeting in the Lord: You should all know that I have granted and quitclaimed to Ranulf, archdeacon of St Andrews, and to his successors in perpetuity, Scoonie, and Balkaithley, and the land that lies on the south side of the city of St Andrews, between the burns, the lands which Walter of blessed memory, archdeacon of the same place, my uncle, held and possessed during his lifetime, which after his time I myself also had and possessed. I have also resigned to the aforesaid archdeacon, Ranulf, the whole right that I had in the aforesaid lands, either from gift, or from grant, or else from confirmation of Bishop Hugh of blessed memory, or my uncle Archdeacon Walter; and I returned the charters which I had concerning those lands to the same aforenamed Ranulf when I knew them to belong to the archdeaconry of St Andrews; and so that this grant and resignation be established and firm for the future, I have reinforced them by the application of the seals of the lord R(alph), bishop of Brechin, and H(enry), abbot of Arbroath, and mine, hanging from the present page, wishing faith to be had in its wording and content in the future. With these witnesses: R(alph), bishop of Brechin; H(enry), abbot of Arbroath, and the religious community of the same place; ... archdeacon of Brechin; William and Peter, chaplain of the lord [bishop] of Brechin; Henry, chaplain of Montrose; Hervey, parson of Inchbraoch; Alan, clerk of Montrose; Roger of Inverkeithing; Herbert of Montrose; William son of Áed; Stephen the Mair; Robert of Hailes; and many others.
